title: OpenAPI 3.0 Specification and Swagger UI

story.as_a: AI agent or developer integrating with loopctl
story.i_want_to: discover all API endpoints, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements from a machine-readable OpenAPI 3.0 specification served by the application itself
story.so_that: I can self-service my integration without reading source code or asking a human for the API contract

rationale: loopctl is an agent-first platform — AI agents are the primary consumers. Agents need a machine-readable API specification to discover endpoints, understand request/response shapes, and authenticate correctly. Without this, every new agent must be manually instructed about the API. An OpenAPI spec also provides interactive Swagger UI for human developers and enables automated client generation.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/openapi returns a valid OpenAPI 3.0 JSON specification document describing all API endpoints, including paths, methods, parameters, request bodies, response schemas, and status codes.
GET /swaggerui serves an interactive Swagger UI HTML page that loads the OpenAPI spec from /api/v1/openapi and allows users to explore and test endpoints in the browser.
GET /api/v1/ returns a JSON welcome response with links to the OpenAPI spec and health endpoint: {"name": "loopctl", "version": "0.1.0", "docs": "/api/v1/openapi", "health": "/health"}.

The OpenAPI spec includes a securitySchemes section defining Bearer API key authentication (type: http, scheme: bearer) and applies it as the default security requirement on all authenticated endpoints.

Every existing controller action (50+ endpoints across tenants, API keys, projects, epics, stories, dependencies, agents, progress, artifacts, verification, orchestrator state, webhooks, admin, import/export, bulk operations, skills) has an OpenApiSpex.operation/2 annotation with summary, description, parameters, request_body, and responses.

Request and response shapes are defined as OpenApiSpex schema modules (e.g., Loopctl.ApiSpec.Schemas.TenantResponse, Loopctl.ApiSpec.Schemas.StoryRequest) that reference the actual field types and validations from the Ecto schemas.

The spec documents all error response shapes: 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 403 (forbidden), 404 (not found), 409 (conflict), 422 (validation error), 429 (rate limited) with consistent error object schema {"error": {"message": string, "status": integer}}.
Pagination parameters (page, page_size) and pagination response metadata (page, page_size, total_count, total_pages) are documented as reusable components referenced by all list endpoints.
The OpenAPI spec is auto-generated from controller annotations — not a static file. Changes to controllers automatically update the spec.
The /api/v1/openapi and /swaggerui endpoints are publicly accessible (no authentication required) so agents can discover the API before registering.
The spec includes x-agent-hints custom extensions on key endpoints describing the typical agent workflow: register → create project → import stories → contract → claim → implement → report → verify.

test_cases.name: OpenAPI spec endpoint returns valid JSON
expected_results: Response body is valid OpenAPI 3.0 (has openapi, info, paths, components keys)

test_cases.name: API root returns welcome response
expected_results: Body includes name, version, docs, health keys

test_cases.name: All controller actions have OpenAPI operations
steps: For each controller, verify OpenApiSpex.operation/2 is defined for every action
expected_results: No controller action is missing an operation spec
The generated spec paths count matches the total route count

test_cases.name: Security scheme is present in spec
Public endpoints (register, health, openapi) have no security requirement
expected_results: These paths have security: [] (empty array, explicitly no auth)

test_cases.name: OpenAPI and Swagger UI accessible without auth

technical_notes: Create lib/loopctl/api_spec.ex as the root ApiSpec module implementing OpenApiSpex.OpenApi behaviour
Create lib/loopctl/api_spec/schemas/ directory for request/response schema modules
Use OpenApiSpex.schema/1 macro in schema modules to define properties from Ecto schema fields
Add `operation/2` callbacks to every controller using `OpenApiSpex.Controller` or manual `open_api_operation/1` functions
Mount OpenApiSpex.Plug.RenderSpec at /api/v1/openapi in the router (outside authenticated scope)
Mount OpenApiSpex.Plug.SwaggerUI at /swaggerui in the router (outside authenticated scope)
Add a simple WelcomeController for GET /api/v1/ with the discovery response
Reusable components: PaginationParams, PaginationMeta, ErrorResponse, TenantResponse, ProjectResponse, StoryResponse, etc.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.
Config-based DI: behaviours resolved via Application.get_env(:loopctl, :key, DefaultModule). config/test.exs maps behaviours to Mox mocks. Tests use expect/3 to set specific expectations.
